repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure_v1: finite hidden-network compatibility proposal did not give the C6/2K3 closure-separator certificate

published_prior_art.cite: aronow_samii_2017_general_interference and savje_2024_misspecified_exposure motivate exposure mappings but do not contain this finite graph-pair separator

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure_v1: Section 11 left finite strict-containment/minimality issues open, but this is a much smaller finite alias witness

published_prior_art.cite: auerbach_guo_tabordmeehan_2026_local_approach: rooted local configurations are broader than the proposed C6/2K3 minimal-closure claim

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship-aspirant minimality claim cites auerbach_guo_tabordmeehan_2026_local_approach without a section, equation, page, or open-problem location for the alleged minimal rooted-closure gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; the kernel is a single finite C6/2K3 alias class tied mainly to one rooted-configuration comparator, not a generic-class frontier or multi-comparator chain.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The necessity clause says any summary measurable only from the marginal law of S gives the same graph-indexed target when eta != 0, but a summary does not define a target unless the induced estimand/equivalence criterion is specified.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: As a flagship-aspirant result, the proof route is only adjacency enumeration plus E[Z_j Z_k]=p^2, so the finite separator is correct but too elementary for flagship tier.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the C6/2K3-only minimality conjecture with a generic class statement for d-regular count-alias graph pairs, with a named observable closure functional and an explicit non-corner witness family.
Add a location-level anchor to the Auerbach-Guo-Tabord-Meehan comparator and state exactly whether the proposal is a strict tightening, an iff frontier, or only a finite illustrative specialization.

Define the count-summary equivalence relation formally: which estimands a summary is allowed to induce, what it means to separate two graph-indexed laws, and why count-measurable summaries necessarily alias the targets.
If aiming for flagship, add a restricted randomization-test or estimator consequence for Hoshino-Yanagi/Gao-Harshaw-Savje-Wang rather than leaving the certificate as a hand-computed graph example.

recommended_next_step: Revise the conjecture into a generic rooted-configuration frontier with a precise equivalence criterion and location-anchored comparator, or downgrade the target tier to field.
